signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

content.text:
## Friday, March 13

### [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade 79-73 Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade-fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/E2025/307/)

Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade put an end to Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul’s nine-game winning streak by defeating the reigning champion 79-73 on Friday night at Belgrade Arena. A first quarter of runs was followed by a second that saw the teams exchange blows before the guests secured a 33-42 lead at halftime. But Zvezda fought back and held Fener to just 14 points by tightening up its defense in the third quarter, before going on a 12-0 surge in the closing stages to get back to winning ways.

Having bounced back from a home loss to FC Bayern Munich last week, the Serbian side improved its record to 18-13. Meanwhile, Fenerbahce snapped a 19-game winning run across all competitions, dropping to 22-8 but staying in first place.

Chima Moneke led all scorers with 21 points, Jordan Nwora scored 14 and Ebuka Izundu had 13, while Codi Miller-McIntyre paired his 10 points with 12 assists and 8 rebounds. Wade Baldwin paced the visitors with 16 points and Bonzie Colson bagged 15, with Talen Horton Tucker netting 14.

Најбољи је осећај кад си Звездаш! ❤️🤍#kkcz #WeAreTheTeam https://t.co/yebRQ3ajpJ pic.twitter.com/Snyb01M2JL

— KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@kkcrvenazvezda) March 13, 2026

### [FC Barcelona 75-80 Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/fc-barcelona-hapoel-ibi-tel-aviv/E2025/310/)

Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv broke a three-game road losing streak by downing FC Barcelona 75-80 at Palau Blaugrana in Round 31 on Friday. Hapoel improved to 18-11, taking a big step towards reaching the playoffs in its first season in the competition. Barca dropped to 17-14 after registering its sixth home loss so far this season. Hapoel now has a 2-0 record over Barca, which would be critical in a tie-break situation.

Dan Oturu led the winners with 22 points on 10-of-11 two-pointers. Elijah Bryant added 21 points, six rebounds and four assists. Kevin Punter paced Barca with 17 points, while Will Clyburn added 15, Tomas Satoransky had 11 and Miles Norris got 10 for the host, which led 26-19 at the end of the first quarter. Bryant and Oturu helped Hapoel get a 40-46 edge at halftime, and then extend the lead to 56-64 late in the third quarter. Barca kept trying but Hapoel managed to keep a safe margin until the final buzzer.

Welcome @KesslerEdwards ❤️ pic.twitter.com/GzSEfMzdv0

— Hapoel Tel Aviv BC (@HapoelTLVBC) March 13, 2026

### [EA7 Emporio Armani Milan 96-87 Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/ea7-emporio-armani-milan-maccabi-rapyd-tel-aviv/E2025/309/)

Milan ground out a hard-fought home win over Maccabi, making it five out of the last seven, after Shavon Shields hit a couple of clutch threes in the home stretch to fend off a spirited fightback from the visiting team. The hosts led for the lion’s share of the contest, by as many as 15 points in the third quarter, but Maccabi kept chipping away and closed in to 84-80 in the latter stages. Then Shields and big man Josh Nebo stepped up to the plate, as Milan improved to 16-15 while the Israeli side dropped to 14-16 after its first loss following four straight wins.

Nebo led the charge with an impressive double-double of 19 points and 10 boards, Shields chipped in with 17 and Zach LeDay netted 15. Quinn Ellis added 12 points while Marko Guduric racked up 11 and 8 assists to underline a flowing team effort. At the other end, Lonnie Walker paced Maccabi on 16 points while Gur Lavi netted 15. Jimmy Clark and Roman Sorkin scored 13 each and O’Shae Brissett amassed 10 points with 12 rebounds.

One last winning game at the Allianz Cloud🤍❤️#ForzaOlimpia pic.twitter.com/iWSFd9BW58

— Olimpia Milano (@OlimpiaMI1936) March 13, 2026

## Thursday, March 12

### [AS Monaco 81-80 Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/as-monaco-olympiacos-piraeus/E2025/306/)

In a thriller that came down to the final shot, Monaco bounced back from recent losses by edging Olympiacos 80-79 at Salle Gaston Mededin. Monaco improved to 17-14 despite only having nine players available and an interim coach, Manuchar Markosihvili, on the sidelines. Olympiacos dropped to 20-11. Matthew Strazel was Monaco's hero after banking in the game-winning floater with 4.0 seconds left.

Mike James paced the winners with 14 points, 5 rebounds and 5 assists before heading to the locker room in the final minute with an apparent leg injury. Jaron Blossomgame and Daniel Theis each added 13 for Monaco. Tyler Dorsey paced Olympiacos with 19 points, Alec Peters added 17, Nikola Milutinov tallied 13 points and 10 rebounds and Tyson Ward scored 10 points for the Reds.

With the game tied at 79-79, Peters split foul shots, Strazel made the go-ahead shot and, after an Olympiacos timeout, Milutinov missed from downtown at the buzzer.

😤 Avec le cœur ❤️‍🔥🇲🇨

🗡 Cette victoire, la #RocaTeam est allée la chercher très, très loin pour battre l'Olympiacos ! 💥#DagheMunegu #EveryGameMatters pic.twitter.com/jnt44oxZbs

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 12, 2026

### [Dubai Basketball 100-94 Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/dubai-basketball-kosner-baskonia-vitoria-gasteiz/E2025/308/)

Dubai recorded an ultimately comfortable win over Baskonia as the hosts led from start to finish and romped ahead by 20 points in the fourth before a late blitz by the Spanish team reduced the arrears in Sarajevo’s Zetra Arena. An early 9-0 run gave Dubai a 15-4 advantage  and the home team never looked back, carving out a 59-45 halftime lead, which soared to 93-73 in the home straight before Baskonia slashed it to 98-94 in the final minute. Dubai celebrated its fifth successive win and improved to 16-14 to stay firmly in contention for a postseason spot, while Baskonia dropped to 9-22 after a sixth successive loss.

Dwayne Bacon led the charge with 23 points while Dzanan Musa chipped in with 19 and 7 boards, and big man Mfiondu Kabengele sank 17 to go with 6 rebounds. McKinley Wright and Filip Petrusev netted 11 points each to garnish a sound team performance. Mamadi Diakite had a monster individual performance for Baskonia as he racked up 27 points and 12 rebounds, while Eugene Omoruyi scored 18 points and Kobi Simmons 14.

Big W in Sarajevo! 5th straight @euroleague win! 🔥🔥🔥 pic.twitter.com/1PHPvor0Vb

— Dubai Basketball (@dubaibasket) March 12, 2026

### [FC Bayern Munich 80-81 Anadolu Efes Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/fc-bayern-munich-anadolu-efes-istanbul/E2025/301/)

Efes picked up a much-needed 80-81 road win over Bayern at SAP Garden to improve to 10-21. Bayern controlled the game through the first half, as it built a lead that reached 13 points. Efes responded in the third quarter with tighter defense and a more consistent scoring rhythm, outscoring Bayern 19-27, with Jordan Loyd contributing 10 points. In the fourth quarter, Efes pulled the game even, and after a tightly contested final few minutes, Cole Swider hit a buzzer-beating three-pointer to give Efes the win.

Efes was led by Loyd with 20 points, while PJ Dozier added 12 points. Şehmus Hazer contributed 11 points, and Ercan Osmani added 10 points and 6 rebounds. For Bayern, Xavier Rathan-Mayes scored 15 points, Andreas Obst added 13 points and David McCormack 9.

👊 Son saniye üçlüğüyle FC Bayern Münih deplasmanında kazanan biz oluyoruz!
FC Bayern Münih 80 - 81 Anadolu Efes | Maç Sonucu#BenimYerimBurası pic.twitter.com/cRkCKj98QZ

— Anadolu Efes SK (@AnadoluEfesSK) March 12, 2026

### [Real Madrid 96-79 Valencia Basket](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/real-madrid-valencia-basket/E2025/303/)

Real Madrid recorded its 15th home victory in 16 games by beating Valencia 96-79 in an all-Spanish League clash at Movistar Arena. A back-and-forth affair in the first quarter was followed by a second in which the hosts outscored Valencia 31-24 for a 51-43 lead at halftime. Madrid hit the ground running in the third quarter and pulled away, 67-48, midway through, but the visitors trimmed the deficit to 75-65 heading into the fourth. Madrid didn’t take its foot off the gas, maintained a comfortable lead and claimed the head-to-head tiebreak advantage over Valencia.

Having secured its fourth straight victory, Real Madrid is now tied with Valencia in the standings with a 21-11 record. Ten Real Madrid players scored at least 6 points, with Mario Hezonja bagging 16 and Gabriel Deck netting 14. Meanwhile, Jean Montero led all scorers with 18 points in a losing effort.

🏁 FP: @RMBaloncesto 96 - 79 @valenciabasket pic.twitter.com/TAiI9emn3m

— Real Madrid Basket (@RMBaloncesto) March 12, 2026

### [Paris Basketball 90-81 LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/paris-basketball-ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/E2025/302/)

Paris overcame ASVEL at Adidas Arena in an entertaining back-and-forth French derby. The 90-81 victory lifted Paris's record to 12-19, while last-placed ASVEL was mathematically eliminated from postseason action.

Though ASVEL set the tone early and took a 9-point lead into halftime, Paris rallied back in the third and eventually earned an advantage of its own. Down the stretch, Justin Robinson enjoyed an offensive explosion that saw him score 12 points in the final 2 minutes.

Robinson played the starring role for Francesco Tabellini’s team, adding 6 assists and 6 boards to a game-changing 25-point scoring performance. Allan Dokossi was a highly-effective supporting act with 12 points and 5 rebounds and Lamar Stevens added 11 points. Glynn Watson’s 14-point and 7-assist performance helped keep ASVEL in the contest.

LA WINNNNNNNNNN
ICI C’EST PARIS ! 😍 pic.twitter.com/4CJ4Ml9nnO

— Paris Basketball (@ParisBasketball) March 12, 2026

### [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens 92-88 Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/panathinaikos-aktor-athens-zalgiris-kaunas/E2025/305/)

Mathias Lessort returned to the floor, and Panathinaikos got back to winning ways after holding off the visiting Zalgiris 92-88. The Greens snapped their four-game losing streak in improving to 17-14 on the season - the same as Zalgiris, but Panathinaikos swept their regular-season series.

All eyes were on Lessort, in his first game back since the 2025 Final Four, and he played a huge part in the victory by playing 23 minutes and collecting 9 points, 3 rebounds, 1 block and 1 key final-minute assist.

Lessort also brought his trademark energy, but when it came to scoring, it was Cedi Osman and Kendrick Nunn doing most of the work. Osman scored 24, while Nunn added 22 points. Kostas Sloukas scored 8 of his 12 points at the end of the third quarter, before Panathinaikos pulled away to a 14-point margin in the fourth. Zalgiris rallied late behind Sylvain Francisco, who finished with 23 points, but it proved to be too little, too late.

HOME WIN! ☘️

Final Score 💪#paobcaktor - @bczalgiris 92-88#WeTheGreens #Club1908 pic.twitter.com/a3WiG462bX

— Panathinaikos BC (@Paobcgr) March 12, 2026

## Wednesday, March 11

### [Virtus Bologna 82-88 Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/virtus-bologna-partizan-mozzart-bet-belgrade/E2025/304/)

Partizan snapped a three-game road winning streak by topping Virtus 82-88 at Virtus Arena in Bologna in an early Round 31 fixture. Partizan improved to 10-20 in the standings while Virtus dropped to 13-18, suffering a significant step back in its race to reach the postseason.

Carlik Jones led the winners with 19 points. Isaac Bonga added 16 points, 11 rebounds and 5 assists for a PIR of 34, Dylan Osetkowski scored 13 points and Tonye Jekiri 10 for Partizan. Carsen Edwards paced Virtus with 19 points, Matt Morgan added 18 and Momo Diouf 14 for the hosts, who trailed 48-52 at halftime and were within 74-77 with over 3 minutes remaining. Jekiri and an unstoppable Jones made the difference down the stretch.

Wednesday is...okay!⚫️⚪️#KKPartizan #EuroLeague pic.twitter.com/KAtgDtZluf

— KK Partizan Mozzart Bet (@PartizanBC) March 11, 2026

heroMedia.title: Round 31 MVP: Cedi Osman, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens

content.text:
A career-high performance in a winning effort allowed [Cedi Osman](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/cedi-osman/004088/) of Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens to earn Round 31 MVP honors with a PIR of 36.

Osman led Panathinaikos to a 92-88 home win over Zalgiris Kaunas with 24 points on 5-of-5 two-pointers, 2-of-2 three-pointers and 8-of-10 free throws, adding five rebounds, three assists, two steals and six fouls drawn for a PIR of 36, his highest in 163 EuroLeague career games. Osman earned MVP of the Round honors for the second time, and is Panathinaikos's third player to take the award this season, after Kendrick Nunn (twice) and Kostas Sloukas.

Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz big man [Mamadi Diakite](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mamadi-diakite/014027/) registered a PIR of 37, the highest in this round, in a 100-94 losing effort against Dubai Basketball. Diakite finished the game with 27 points on 7-of-9 two-pointers, 2-of-6 three-pointers and 7-of-7 free throws, plus 12 rebounds, an assist, a steal and seven fouls drawn. [Isaac Bonga](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/isaac-bonga/006760/) of Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade amassed a PIR of 34 in his team's 82-88 road victory over Virtus Bologna. Bonga had 16 points on 3-of-4 two-pointers, 2-of-4 three-pointers and 4-of-4 free throws, 11 rebounds, five assists, two blocks and six fouls drawn.

[Elijah Bryant](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/elijah-bryant/009846/) of Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv and [Justin Robinson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/justin-robinson/014008/) of Paris Basketball, each with a PIR of 32, complete the list of top individual performances in Round 31. Bryant led Hapoel to a 75-80 road win over FC Barcelona with 21 points, six rebounds, four assists and six fouls drawn. Robinson helped Paris beat LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne 90-81 with 25 points, six rebounds, six assists and six fouls drawn.

### Individual highs: Codi Miller-McIntyre of Zvezda, 12 assists

Diakite was this week's top scorer with 27 points against Dubai. Nunn leads the overall standings at 19.5 points per night.

Diakite and O'Shae Brissett of Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv each pulled down 12 rebounds in Round 31. Nikola Milutinov of Olympiacos Piraeus is the EuroLeague's top rebounder at 7.3 per game.

Codi Miller-McIntyre of Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade was the only player to get double-digits in assists, dishing 12 against Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul. He also leads the overall standings with 7.1 assists per game.

PJ Dozier of Anadolu Efes Istanbul collected four steals in his team's last-second 80-81 road win over FC Bayern Munich. Aleksa Avramovic of Dubai is first at 1.6 steals per night.

Neal Sako of Valencia Basket and Walter Tavares of Real Madrid both had three blocks in this round. Tavares ranks first overall at 2.0 blocks per game.

Six players registered double-doubles in Round 31: Diakite (27 points, 12 rebounds), Bonga (16 points, 11 rebounds), Brissett (10 points, 12 rebounds), Miller-McIntyre (10 points, 12 assists), Milutinov (13 points, 10 rebounds) and Josh Nebo (19 points, 10 rebounds) of EA7 Emporio Armani Milan.

articleMetadata.description: A career-high performance in a winning effort allowed Cedi Osman of Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens to earn Round 31 MVP honors with a PIR of 36. Osman led Panathinaikos to a 92-88 home win over Zalgiris Kaunas with 24 points on 5-of-5 two-pointers, 2-of-2 three-pointers and 8-of-10 free throws, adding five rebounds, three assists, two steals and six fouls drawn for a PIR of 36, his highest in 163 EuroLeague career games.

heroMedia.title: Josh Nebo slays former club as Milan beats battling Maccabi
heroMedia.summary: The big man posted a double-double to boost the Italian side’s postseason hopes

linkedIds.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-03-13T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
EA7 Emporio Armani Milan engineered a lifeline 96-87 home win over Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv to stay in the hunt for a play-in berth, while denting the Israeli side’s hopes of squeezing into the business end of Europe’s premier club competition.

The Italian team improved to 16-15 after grinding out its fifth win in the last seven games as Maccabi dropped to 14-16 after suffering a first defeat in five outings.

Center Josh Nebo put his former club Maccabi to the sword with a monster performance of 19 points and 10 rebounds, and Shavon Shields netted 11 of his 17 points in the fourth quarter as Milan repelled a fierce Maccabi fightback.

The visitors had slashed a 15-point third quarter deficit to just 84-80 in the home stretch before Shields sank a pair of successive three-pointers to put the game beyond Maccabi’s reach. Marko Guduric racked up 11 points and 8 assists for the home team, Zach LeDay scored 15 and Quinn Ellis added 12 as Milan gave itself a fighting chance of sneaking into the top 10 at the end of the Regular Season.

Nebo, who was a handful for his opponents all night, acknowledged that keeping their nerve when the chips were down in the fourth quarter was crucial for the home players.  

“In the past we had some trouble in the fourth quarter and today was a big step forward in responding to their run and closing the game out,” Nebo said in his postgame comments. “It was a tough game and we’re trying to push for a spot in the playoffs, so I try to bring my best effort to every matchup. The race is very tight so we’re trying to take it one game at a time and if we keep fighting like we did tonight, we’ve got a good chance,” he added.

Maccabi nosed ahead 25-26 at the end of the first quarter as Tamir Blatt beat the buzzer with an audacious three-pointer from the halfway line, but Milan then gained the upper hand and led 53-42 at halftime. The host appeared to be in for some plain sailing when it romped 59-44 ahead early in the third, but Maccabi had other ideas and always threatened to force a late twist before Shields delivered for Milan from long range.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Olimpia Milano (@olimpiamilano1936)

The home team’s head coach Pepe Poeta was able to breathe a huge sigh of relief on the final buzzer after seeing his men close out the game. “We had three very solid quarters and we handled the endgame in a proper way,” he said. “It’s impossible to take away everything from a team as talented on offense as Maccabi but we did a good job limiting them and with this spirit we will try to reach the postseason.”

Lonnie Walker paced the visitors on 16 points and Gur Lavi, who averaged just 3.1 points per game before Friday night, served up a big first half when he poured in 13 of his 15 points, eventually finishing the game on a perfect 7-of-7 from the floor. Roman Sorkin and Jimmy Clark scored 13 apiece for Maccabi but the visitors were ultimately undone by Milan’s sound free throw shooting, as the home team netted 27 of 30 from the foul line.

“We played against a good team and they shot the ball really well,” Maccabi head coach Oded Kattash said. “We did a better job in the second half and the most important thing is that we kept fighting. We have a week to recover and get the next one.”

heroMedia.title: Codi Miller-McIntyre flips the switch as Zvezda ends Fenerbahce’s winning run
heroMedia.summary: The 31-year-old guard brought the energy in the third quarter as Zvezda’s defense suffocated the reigning champion

linkedIds.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (2026-03-13T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade’s home loss to FC Bayern Munich left behind a wounded beast in search of a bounce-back display that would put the team back on track. And what a better way to kick into gear than by ending the reigning champion’s nine-game winning streak, at the very same arena where they ran into trouble last week.

Zvezda relied on its physicality and suffocating defense in the second half to beat Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul 79-73 at Belgrade Arena on Friday night.

“We went through big pressure,” Sasa Obradovic admitted in his post-game flash interview, recalling the previous week's setback. “The Bayern game put us in a so-so situation, but with a great character, with a great attitude, approach, we did a great job.”

Leading the charge was Codi Miller-McIntyre, who pulled off an all-around performance, bringing the energy when Zvezda needed an injection of intensity in their bid to turn the game around.

The hosts started strong, taking a 10-2 lead, but Fenerbahce soon bounced back to establish a narrow lead before taking a 33-42 advantage into halftime.

At this point, Zvezda looked dull on both ends of the floor, failing to find rhythm on offense and struggling to contain Wade Baldwin and Bonzie Colson, who combined for 22 points before the break.

The guests had the Serbian side exactly where they wanted them, but Miller-McIntyre decided it was time to change gears. Zvezda hit the ground running in the second half, trimming the deficit to 43-46 after a bucket from Jordan Nwora in transition.

With the fans on their feet after Nwora’s layup, the decibels skyrocketed in the arena. Fenerbahce tried to respond on the other end, but Miller-McIntyre put pressure on the ball, got the steal and detonated a two-handed slam in transition.

Saras Jasikevicius, who had warned before the game that his team should not “let the crowd in the game”, called a timeout in his bid to end Zvezda’s momentum. Fener replied with a triple from Colson, but Miller-McIntyre was locked in on both ends of the floor.

Following a miss from Nando De Colo, he picked up the rebound, went coast-to-coast, skied for another ferocious slam and drew a foul from Talen Horton Tucker, trimming the deficit to 48-49.

Zvezda had not even taken the lead and the crowd was buzzing. The momentum had shifted.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@crvenazvezdakk)

“Like I always say, if we come out with defensive energy, we can beat everybody or have a chance to win any game and that’s what we did in the third quarter,” Miller-McIntyre told EuroLeague TV. “We played a great defensive game.”

Zvezda held Fenerbahce to just 14 points in the third quarter, and 18 in the fourth. The visitors pushed back and went on a 0-9 run midway through the closing period, but Zvezda answered with a 12-0 surge, fueled by Jared Butler and Nwora, to finish the game off.

“A typical playoff game I would say,” Obradovic added. “We raised our aggressiveness in the second half, in defense especially, and that’s why we won the game. On the other hand, a tough opponent. Every possession matters [and] it really did, but we found a way like many other times. Having great energy, maybe we can beat anybody.”

Miller-McIntyre was 2 rebounds shy of completing a triple-double, as he netted 10 points, recorded 12 assists and downed 8 boards in a complete outing.

Having rediscovered its confidence by forcing Fener to its first loss in 20 games across all competitions, Zvezda will now look to consolidate its position in the playoff spots. The competition is fierce in an exciting battle for homecourt advantage in the playoffs, but the Serbian side proved on Friday that it has what it takes to beat literally any EuroLeague side – even the high-flying reigning champion.

heroMedia.title: Dan Oturu, Elijah Bryant lead Hapoel to critical win
heroMedia.summary: Despite a late scratch, two key players took Hapoel to its 18th win

linkedIds.text: FC Barcelona v Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (2026-03-13T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
It was a strange night at Palau Blaugrana, for a game between FC Barcelona and Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv played behind closed doors in which both teams faced last-minute injury problems. Barca was without star power forward Toko Shengelia, while Hapoel arrived in the building without center Johnathan Motley, who had been dealing with an ankle sprain in recent days and could not recover in time for Hapoel's first game in 14 days, further adding to the unpredictable nature of the contest.

Visiting coach Dimitris Itoudis was in uncharted territory, as Motley had taken part in all of his team's 28 EuroLeague games until now. A quick analysis, however, revealed who would become the most likely go-to guys.

During the last three games in which Motley played less than 10 minutes, Elijah Bryant and Dan Oturu had posted really solid numbers. In those games against FC Bayern Munich, Anadolu Efes Istanbul and EA7 Emporio Armani Milan, Bryant averaged 17.3 points, 6.0 rebounds, 4.3 assists and a PIR of 26.0, while Oturu had delivered 13.3 points on 65.5% two-point shooting, along with 6.7 rebounds and a PIR of 15.7.

Sticking with that script, both players delivered again at Palau Blaugrana. Oturu had 11 points during a first quarter in which he appeared to be the only visitor playing with the right level of energy. Hapoel only committed one foul in the first quarter, allowing Barca to hit 10-of-14 two-pointers (71.4%), while dishing six assists with just one turnover.

"We started rusty, let's be honest," Itoudis admitted after the game. "We started rusty and I think that was a lack of official games. But we used it after that very well. In two quarters, we gave up 14 points - the second quarter and the last quarter. The problems for us were turnovers. We turned the ball over too much and missed free throws. But other than that, we had a clear target of what to do."

Everything changed in the second quarter, which Hapoel's second unit opened with a 0-10 run to make Barca call timeout at 26-29. Levi Randolph, Kessler Edwards and Chris Jones came off the bench with the right mindset and once Bryant, Oturu and Vasilije Micic returned to the floor, Hapoel brought out its best basketball.

The visitor subsequently led 40-46 at halftime and quickly extended its margin after the break: Bryant hit a three-pointer, Micic followed that with a driving layup and Oturu's basket made it a double-digit game, 54-64. At the end of the third quarter, Bryant had 19 points, no missed shots, four rebounds, three assists and a PIR of 29.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by 𝗛𝗮𝗽𝗼𝗲𝗹 𝗧𝗲𝗹 𝗔𝘃𝗶𝘃 𝗕𝗖 (@hapoeltlvbc)

Barca only got closer once the outcome was already clear, and the stats make it clear who mattered the most: Oturu finished the game with 22 points on 10-of-11 two-pointers, while Bryant had 21 points, six defensive rebounds, four assists and a PIR of 32. Hapoel's collective defensive effort was also huge in the last three quarters, in which Barca went 17-of-34 inside the arc (50% 2FG) and 2-of-16 from downtown (12.5% 3FG), getting just five assists in the last 30 minutes against seven turnovers.

"We had a good point of emphasis in the paint, good perimeter movement of the ball, almost 20 assists. And I think we were a better team," Itoudis said. "So congratulations to the team, playing and winning on the road, in Barcelona. It says a lot. And the fact that we are staying in the chase of the play-in spot, it says something for this team."

heroMedia.title: Monaco rocked by James injury blow
heroMedia.summary: EuroLeague’s all-time leading scorer faces a spell on the sidelines

content.text:
AS Monaco’s playoff hopes have been hit with confirmation that superstar Mike James will miss two or three weeks of action with a hamstring injury.

The all-time EuroLeague scoring leader suffered the injury during the penultimate minute of his team’s dramatic win over Olympiacos Piraeus on Thursday, and the extent of the problem was confirmed by the club after tests on Friday.

James will miss next week’s trip to Anadolu Efes Istanbul and must be considered highly doubtful for the subsequent double round week at the end of March, when Monaco will face a pair of direct rivals in the race for a top ten finish: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan at home, followed by a trip to Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens.

The veteran playmaker leads Monaco in both scoring (16.7 points per game) and assists (6.6 per night), and his absence will place further pressure on an already-undermanned roster which contained just nine players for the victory over Olympiacos.

- [Laurynas Birutis is sidelined with a knee problem](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/laurynas-birutis-sidelined-with-knee-pain/). He missed Round 30 but has made the trip to Athens for Round 31.

## Wednesday, March 11

### Virtus Bologna vs. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (20:45 CET)

##### Virtus Bologna

Daniel Hackett and Alessandro Pajola are ruled out for Virtus's game against Partizan on Wednesday, the club has confirmed.

- Daniel Hackett suffered an injury during the Italian Cup and has been out since Round 29.
- [Alessandro Pajola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/alessandro-pajola-out-indefinitely-with-knee-injury/), who has been out since Round 25, is sidelined indefinitely with a knee injury.

##### Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade

Duane Washington, Vanja Marinkovic and Mario Nakic are ruled out for Partizan's game at Virtus on Wednesday, the club has confirmed. 

- Duane Washington is sidelined for the coming weeks with a calf injury, [the club announced on March 7](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/partizan-loses-duane-washington-to-calf-injury/).
- Shake Milton, out since Round 21 with a broken hand, returned to action in Round 29.
- Carlik Jones, out since Round 5 with an ankle injury, returned to action in Round 29. 
- Vanja Marinkovic [suffered an Achilles injury during the comeback win over Hapoel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/partizans-vanja-marinkovic-out-long-term-with-achilles-injury/) and is out for the season.
- Mario Nakic has been sidelined with a knee problem.
- [Jabari Parker has left the club on loan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/jabari-parker-departs-partizan-on-loan/).

heroMedia.summary: All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at euroleaguef4tickets.com.

content.text:
The road to the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad continues with unprecedented global demand, as ticket sales enter their third public phase on March 17 at 10:00 CET.

Following two completely sold-out public sales windows, Phase 3 will offer fans another opportunity to secure their seats for European basketball’s premier event, set to take place in Athens.

Phase 1, held on February 11, saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform, with all available tickets selling out in less than five minutes. Phase 2, which opened on February 26, was also fully sold out shortly after launch, further reaffirming the extraordinary appeal and global reach of the EuroLeague’s showcase event.

The overwhelming demand highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to witness the season’s ultimate showdown live.

The third public ticket sales phase will officially open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET. Each remaining sales phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is once again expected.

The final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, strategically positioned coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season, as ten teams remain in the race for a Final Four spot.

All phases follow a fair access policy: fans who purchase tickets in one phase are not eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure ensures broader access to the event. Each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets per transaction.

Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), before the season culminates with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [euroleaguef4tickets.com](http://euroleaguef4tickets.com).

On Friday night Mathias Lessort made a long-awaited return to the basketball floor. In his first game in nearly 10 months, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens center took center stage in his team’s win over Zalgiris Kaunas.

Ready to go from the tip off, and with support from his family sitting courtside, Lessort looked like his old self. heroMedia.title: Mathias Lessort returns in style as Panathinaikos downs Zalgiris
heroMedia.summary: The Greens snapped a four-game losing streak

linkedIds.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Zalgiris Kaunas (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Mathias Lessort is finally back! And immediately making a difference.

For the first time since the 2025 Final Four, and nearly 15 months removed from a gruesome leg injury, the former all-EuroLeague center returned to basketball action. In his emotional return, with his family sitting courtside, Lessort was a key cog as Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens snapped a four-game losing skid and downed visiting Zalgiris Kaunas 92-88 on Thursday night.

Cedi Osman scored 24 points on perfect shooting from the field, and Kendrick Nunn netted 22 points. But all eyes were on Lessort, and for all the good reasons.

“He is the heart and soul of this team”, Osman said about his teammate. “We know how much work he put in in this year and a half. We are really happy that he is back. He did a great job today. From the first play, he brought the energy from the beginning and that’s what we were looking for.”

After a lengthy absence, it was not clear how much the big man would be able to contribute. But if this one game was any indication, Lessort will quickly return to being a centerpiece of everything the Greens do.

Lessort started the game, scored after 22 seconds, had a highlight-reel dunk after 90 seconds, and ended up playing 23 minutes and 19 seconds in which he collected 9 points, 3 rebounds, 1 block and a key final-minute assist.

“I am tired, but I am happy”, Lessort told EuroLeague TV interview with his recognizable big smile. “For me, it was the most important to contribute for the team win. It was a crucial moment for us, and we need to start with the wins.”

Not coincidentally, with Lessort the paint, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens looked a bit more like its old self.

“We moved the ball well, and played more comfortable with Mathias,” Coach Ergin Ataman said.

A quick look at the stats tells that Panathinaikos attempted 50 two-pointers, and scored more than 90 points despite attempting only 11 triples. It also outrebounded Zalgiris 30-23 in the final three quarters.

It's okay to interrupt interviews like that 🫂 pic.twitter.com/lb8OzJIz6F

— EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) March 12, 2026

Ataman stressed that the key was the return of Lessort. And the Panathinaikos coach was so confident in his center’s abilities despite a long absence that he did not shy away from making him a big part of the game plan.

“Zalgiris’s most important player is Moses Wright, and we wanted to get him in foul problems early, we planned to attack him from the beginning with Mathias. Because I saw in practice that he is ready to attack, and we know he can attack very well in the low post,” Ataman explained. “[Lessort] made a great job, and finally after a year and a half, we found our regular game, playing inside-outside basketball game.”

Panathinaikos took the lead for good in the second quarter behind Lessort’s effort and Osman’s baskets. Kostas Sloukas scored 8 consecutive points to end the third quarter, then Nunn and Osman extended the margin to 14 points in the fourth.

However, Sylvain Francisco rallied Zalgiris, which cut it to 85-82 entering the final minute. On the ensuing Panathinaikos possession, the ball went inside to Lessort, who showed no sign of his long absence in game’s key moment, finding Juancho Hernangomez alone on the wing, as the Spaniard made his first basket of the night with 45 seconds to go, sealing the outcome.

Lessort was well aware that he would be the story everyone was be talking about after the game, but he stressed that he wanted to make the night about the team. And he succeeded.

“I tried to execute my routine and approach the game like I approached them before I got injured”, Lessort explained. “Obviously, it was a lot of adrenaline, a lot of rush going through my body. But I tried to calm down, tried to tell myself to approach like I approached the game before. I did not want this game to be about me, I wanted it to be about the team and about the win we need to get. I didn’t try to show something, I didn’t try to be the hero, I was just trying to be a teammate and help my team get a win.”

This win improved Panathinaikos's record to 17-14, tied with Zalgiris. But with seven games to go, the head-to-head sweep Panathinaikos now holds over Zalgiris could play a big role in the final standings. And with Lessort back, and likely only to improve as he gets his legs under him, the Greens have everything to once again become the team to beat.

heroMedia.title: Paris overcomes ASVEL thanks to Justin Robinson’s late heroics
heroMedia.summary: A massive fourth quarter surge from Robinson powers Paris to a comeback win after a shaky first half

linkedIds.text: Paris Basketball v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
“I think it’s a very valuable win, for many reasons,” said Francesco Tabellini, moments after his Paris Basketball team earned a 90-81 win over fellow French side LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne. “I’m super proud of my players and the way they picked it up.”  
The Italian head coach had plenty to be proud of in the second half, but it wasn’t a straightforward victory for the Parisians.

Last-placed ASVEL set the tone in the opening quarter, executing well to finish the opening period with a 6-point advantage. The visiting side kept its foot on the gas in the second, moving the ball slickly and controlling the glass to take a 9-point lead into the halftime break.

The hosts came back swinging in the second half, rallying back with a 7-0 run early in the third period to carve a route back into the contest. Though ASVEL offered some resistance, Paris poured in the points, eventually putting together a 29-point quarter and taking control of the game.

Pierric Poupet’s team fought back down the stretch, but the home team couldn’t be stopped. Paris guard Justin Robinson exploded for 12 points in the final 2 minutes of the game to propel his team over the line. When the final buzzer sounded, they had secured a 9-point victory.

The 28-year-old Robinson was the undeniable star of the show, adding 6 assists and 6 boards to a game-changing 25-point scoring performance. His back-to-back clutch threes in the fourth made a huge difference, but Robinson attributed the plays to the confidence given to him by his teammates.

“They were daggers,” said Robinson. “You can hear my teammates in the background, they give me confidence all the time. I started off slow, missed a lot of threes, but made them when it mattered. My coaches and teammates trusted me to step up big. I stepped up to the challenge and made big shots down the stretch.”

Having impressed during his first season in the French capital, Robinson seems to be enjoying every moment.

“This is who I am as a character and as a player,” Robinson shared. “I like to have fun. In those big moments down the stretch, I just try to get the crowd hyped, my teammates pumped up, and just keep going. It’s all fun and games, I’m blessed to play this game, in this beautiful league.”

Of course, Robinson couldn’t win the game alone. Allan Dokossi was a highly effective supporting act, recording 12 points and 5 rebounds and Lamar Stevens added 11 points. Overall, Tabellini was encouraged by the way his team performed in the end.

“We started the game pretty poorly, shooting-wise, and lost a bit of our trust. Then we kept our composure and didn’t allow them to take a big lead, so in the second half it was completely different, we put together great offense, it was amazing.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Paris Basketball 🏀 (@parisbasketball)

On the other bench, Poupet was understandably disappointed with the loss, but he sees it as yet another learning opportunity.

“We had a great first half, then a good 5 minutes at the end, but it wasn’t enough,” Poupet said. “We had a long stretch without scoring or making stops. We have to learn from it, we cannot play 25 good minutes, we have to make it 30, 35 or 40 maybe, so that we can win games.”

Poupet may hope for significant improvements, but ASVEL will now play for a combination of pride and future development. The loss moves his team to 8-23 for the season and mathematically eliminates it from playoff qualification.

Paris’s record improves to 12-19, as it holds onto an outside chance of postseason action. Tabellini’s men will face their next challenge on Tuesday, when they meet Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv.

content.text:
## Friday, March 6

### [Anadolu Efes Istanbul 88-91 LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/anadolu-efes-istanbul-ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/E2025/297/)

ASVEL snapped a three-game losing streak by holding off Efes 88-91 on Friday night. This was ASVEL’s fifth straight win over Efes which improved the team to a league-worst 8-22 record, while Efes dropped to 9-21.

ASVEL led for the majority of the night but never more than 8 points in the second half. Behind Braian Angola and his personal-best 26 points, the visitors held off Efes’s late-game charge to pick up their second road win of the season.

Angola scored 26 points, Zac Seljaas netted 15 and Thomas Heurtel had 14 points and 7 assists in the win. Sehmus Hazer and his career-high 20 points were the main reason why Efes came back into the game. Saben Lee was a force with 18 points, 5 rebounds and 4 assists, while Rolands Smits scored 12 in defeat.

ON NE LÂCHERA RIEN !

Superbe performance collective de nos Villeurbannais et victoire de prestige sur le parquet de l’Anadolu Efes !

Bravo les gars ! pic.twitter.com/bpmvvyVXge

— LDLC ASVEL (@LDLCASVEL) March 6, 2026

### [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade 80-85 FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade-fc-bayern-munich/E2025/299/)

Bayern improved to 13-17 with a shock road win at Zvezda, which dropped to 17-13 and suffered a potentially damaging defeat in its bid to reach the playoffs after shooting a miserable 6-of-30 from deep. The hosts trailed for the lion’s share of the contest but looked like turning the tide after taking a 78-75 late in the game. However, Zvezda was ice-cold from the floor in the home straight and Bayern took advantage to close out the game with a 2-10 run which silenced the raucous home crown in a jam-packed Belgrade Arena.

Andi Obst paced Bayern on 16 points despite shooting just 2-of-8 from downtown while Xavier Rathan-Mayes added 13 after a huge second half and Neno Dimitrijevic chipped in with 11. David McCormack netted 10 and racked up 4 rebounds. At the other end, Chima Moneke poured 22 points while Jared Butler and Jordan Nwora scored 10 apiece, the latter shooting 0-of-6 from long range.

𝐖𝐈𝐍 𝐀𝐆𝐀𝐈𝐍𝐒𝐓 𝐑𝐄𝐃 𝐒𝐓𝐀𝐑 💪 #CZVBAY pic.twitter.com/MTQnSmqPTW

— FC Bayern Basketball (@FCBBasketball) March 6, 2026

### [Olympiacos Piraeus 86-80 Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/olympiacos-piraeus-panathinaikos-aktor-athens/E2025/300/)

Olympiacos extended its winning streak in the Greek derby to 11 games by beating Panathinaikos 86-80 at the Peace and Friendship Stadium on Friday night. A 9-2 run midway through the first quarter saw the hosts take a 20-13 lead heading into the second. What followed was a back-and-forth affair, with the hosts taking a 40-29 advantage at halftime. Panathinaikos bounced back and turned the game into a thriller by outscoring the Reds 18-27 in the third quarter. Yet, Olympiacos kept its composure in crunch time despite the key absences of Thomas Walkup and Sasha Vezenkov as it swept its regular-season series over the Greens.

Having gotten back to winning ways following a loss at Zalgiris last week, Georgios Bartzokas’s side improved its record to 19-10. Meanwhile, Panathinaikos suffered its fourth straight loss, dropping to 16-14. As many as 10 Olympiacos players got their names on the scoresheet, with Tyler Dorsey netting 13 points and Nikola Milutinov bagging 12. Kendrick Nunn scored a game-high 20 points, with Cedi Osman and Juancho Hernangomez pouring in 14 each.

FINAL 🏀 #OlympiacosBC - Panathinaikos 86-80 #EveryGameMatters #WeAreOlympiacos #TogetherWeFight #OLYPAO pic.twitter.com/GIU4yhk2dk

— Olympiacos B.C. (@Olympiacos_BC) March 6, 2026

### [Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz 81-97 Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/kosner-baskonia-vitoria-gasteiz-paris-basketball/E2025/298/)

Paris picked up a dominant 81-97 victory over Baskonia on Friday night at Buesa Arena, extending its winning streak to three games and claiming its third consecutive road win to move to 11-18. After starting the first quarter by quickly taking advantage of fastbreak opportunities and strong three-point shooting, Paris completely took control of the momentum in the second quarter, stretching the lead to as many as 25 points. In the second half, Paris continued to find easy baskets, and never looked back.

Nadir Hifi led the way with 21 points, going 5-of-7 on two-pointers and a perfect 8-of-8 from the free-throw line. Justin Robinson added 19 points, hitting 4 three-pointers, while Jared Rhoden scored 16, Lamar Stevens 11, and Allan Dokossi finished with 11 rebounds and 6 assists. For Baskonia, Eugene Omoruyi led with 17 points, Timothe Luwawu-Cabarrot scored 13, Clement Frisch added 13, and Kobi Simmons chipped in with 12 points.

Solid performance for @ParisBasketball in Vitoria!#EveryGameMatters pic.twitter.com/se5p6fT2MF

— EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) March 6, 2026

## Thursday, March 5

### [Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul 88-70 AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul-as-monaco/E2025/292/)

Fenerbahce stretched its winning streak to nine games by beating Monaco 88-70 at Ulker Sports and Event Hall. A 9-0 run gave Fenerbahce an early 12-4 lead, with the hosts dictating the tempo and suffocating Monaco with their defense from start to finish. The Turkish side pulled away by holding the guests to 16 points in the third quarter and Talen Horton-Tucker stepped up in the fourth to put the game to bed.

Having clinched its third straight victory over Vassilis Spanoulis's side, Fenerbahce improved its record to 22-7. Meanwhile, a struggling Monaco lost for the seventh time in eight games, dropping to 16-14.

THT ended the game on 28 points, just 1 shy from his career high. Having gone 4-for-6 from deep, Bonzie Colson bagged 14 points. Mike James paced Monaco with 14 points and Jaron Blossomgame netted 12 in a losing effort.

EuroLeague’de Bir Galibiyet Daha! 💪

Tebrikler Fenerbahçe Beko! 👏

Maç Sonucu | Fenerbahçe Beko 🆚 AS Monaco: 88-70

Skor dağılımımız: Horton-Tucker 28, Colson 14, Jantunen 10, Baldwin 10, Silva 7, Biberovic 10, Birch 4, Boston Jr 3, Bacot 2. #YellowLegacy pic.twitter.com/ADKq5Sf786

— Fenerbahçe Beko (@FBBasketbol) March 5, 2026

### [Valencia Basket 91-87 Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/valencia-basket-zalgiris-kaunas/E2025/296/)

Valencia secured a hard-fought 91-87 victory over Zalgiris at Roig Arena on Thursday night, improving its record to 20-10 and maintaining second place in the standings. With the loss, Zalgiris’s two-game winning streak came to an end, dropping it to 17-13 and putting its top-six standing at risk.

The hosts came out strong, demonstrating their offensive strength in the first half and taking a double-digit lead (50-39) into halftime. After the break, Zalgiris tightened its defense and reduced Valencia's lead to just two points, 58-56, midway through the third quarter. The visitors later tied the game at 87-87 heading into the final minute. However, the Lithuanian team couldn't pull ahead as Brancou Badio hit a contested baseline jumper to put Valencia 89-87 ahead, and Jean Montero sealed the win from the free-throw line after Sylvain Francisco missed a potential game-winning three-pointer. 

Omari Moore led Valencia with 16 points in 21 minutes, Nate Reuvers followed him with 12 points on a perfect shooting night, while Badio added 11 and Jaime Pradilla 10 for the winners. Moses Wright neared a double-double with 20 points and 9 boards, Dustin Sleva contributed 14 points and Ignas Brazdeikis poured in 13 points on 3-of-3 from behind the arc in the losing effort.

¡Tumbamos a un equipazo! 😍

🔥 2️⃣0️⃣ 𝙑𝙄𝘾𝙏𝙊𝙍𝙄𝘼𝙎 𝙀𝙉 𝙀𝙐𝙍𝙊𝙇𝙀𝘼𝙂𝙐𝙀 🔥

¡Qué locura! 🤯
¡Seguimos, familia!

VAMOOOOOOOOOOOS 🧡

🏆 J30 @EuroLeague
🟠 @valenciabasket 91
🟢 @bczalgiris 87
🤝 @Heineken_ES 0,0 pic.twitter.com/cHMeC2n6pE

— Valencia Basket Club (@valenciabasket) March 5, 2026

### [EA7 Emporio Armani Milan 87-84 FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/ea7-emporio-armani-milan-fc-barcelona/E2025/291/)

Milan escaped a dramatic late Barcelona comeback to earn a nervy win at Allianz Cloud on Thursday night. The 87-84 victory moves the Italian team’s record to 15-15 and snaps a two-game losing streak, while the Catalan side, having now lost five of its  last six games, fell to 17-13 as the fight for playoff basketball continues.

Milan earned an early lead in the opening quarter and built on it in the second. The key period was the third however, when the hosts ran away with the contest by widening the margin to a huge 27 points (78-51). Barcelona pushed back in the final quarter, as Milan showed real signs of sluggishness and turned the ball over with consistency. Amazingly, Barca made it a two-point game, but it wasn’t enough and Milan held on for the unjustifiably narrow W.

Zach LeDay played the starring role, pairing 16 points with 7 boards. Leandro Bolmaro also made his mark on the game, recording 13 points, 4 rebounds and 6 assists. Quinn Ellis’s defensive effort paid off, as he combined 3 steals with his 5 assists. Bolstering the offense further, Devin Booker, Armoni Brooks and Josh Nebo also finished the clash on double figures.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Olimpia Milano (@olimpiamilano1936)

### [Real Madrid 92-84 Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/game-center/2025-26/real-madrid-virtus-bologna/E2025/295/)

Real ground out a valuable home win over Virtus as Mario Hezonja sank 8 of his 19 points in the home stretch, forcing the Italian side into submission after the contest seemed to be heading for a dramatic finish with the teams tied 77-77 deep in the fourth quarter. Virtus erased an 11-point deficit in the second half and led 69-72 but the hosts had more left in the tank when the chips were down, improving to 19-11 while the visitors dropped to 13-17.

Hezonja added 6 boards and as many assists to his tally. Walter Tavares chipped in with 14 points and 5 blocks and Facundo Campazzo scored 12 for Real, which shot 11-of-28 from deep after missing its first 5 long-range efforts. Carsen Edwards netted 22 of his game-high 32 points for Virtus in the first half, Luca Vildoza sank 15 and Derrick Alston amassed 11 in defeat.

🏁 FP: @RMBaloncesto 92 - 84 @VirtusBo pic.twitter.com/N50ouvQj3Z

— Real Madrid Basket (@RMBaloncesto) March 5, 2026
